    L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanNextToken_flow_close_mapping_outermost_ext
    L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanFiltered_boundary_tokens
    L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanFlowSequenceStart_filtered
    L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanFlowMappingStart_filtered
    L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanFlowEntry_filtered
    L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.ScanChain_deterministic
    L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.ScanChain.split
    L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.emitList_body_filtered_characterization
    L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.emitPairList_body_filtered_characterization
    L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanFiltered_emitSeq_nonempty_structure
    L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanFiltered_emitMap_nonempty_structure
    L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.parseStream_emitSequence
    L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.parseStream_emitMapping
    L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.parseStream_accepts_emit_tokens
    L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.emit_produces_single_document
    L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.emit_parse_succeeds
    L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.emit_parseYaml_succeeds
    L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.contentEq_sequence_items
    L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.contentEq_mapping_pairs
    L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.contentEq_seq_style_irrel
    L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.contentEq_map_style_irrel
    L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.emit_roundtrip_sequence_content_eq
    L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.emit_roundtrip_mapping_content_eq
    L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.emit_roundtrip_content_eq
    L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.universal_roundtrip

    Emitter Scannability (Phase E, Steps 1–2) #

    Step 1 — Proof that the canonical emitter's output is accepted by the scanner:

    ∀ v, Grammable v false → ∃ tokens, Scanner.scanFiltered (emit v) = .ok tokens

    Step 2 — Composition with the parser to prove the full pipeline succeeds:

    ∀ v, Grammable v false → ∃ docs, parseYamlRaw (emit v) = .ok docs

    Architecture #

    The canonical emitter produces a strict subset of YAML:

    • All scalars are double-quoted ("...")
    • All sequences are flow-style ([...])
    • All mappings are flow-style ({...})
    • No block constructs, no plain scalars, no document markers

    Proof Strategy #

    Rather than reasoning about the scanner's state machine directly, we prove that parseYamlRaw (emit v) = .ok docs for all grammable v. This is equivalent to proving both scanner acceptance and parser success.

    The proof proceeds by structural induction on YamlValue:

    §1 — Escape character validity: each escapeChar c produces output that collectDoubleQuotedLoop accepts. §2 — Emitter output properties: non-emptiness and structural facts. §3 — Scanner acceptance (Step 1): scan_accepts_emitScalar and emit_produces_valid_yaml. §4 — Full pipeline composition (Step 2): parse acceptance, single-document guarantee, and grammability preservation.

    Zero Axioms #

    Target: all theorems machine-checked with 0 sorry, 0 axiom, 0 admit.

    §1 Escape Character Properties #

    The emitter's escapeChar function produces output that is valid for the scanner's collectDoubleQuotedLoop. We need two properties:

    1. Characters that are escaped (e.g., \n, \\, \") produce valid two-character escape sequences recognized by processEscape.
    2. Characters that pass through unchanged are nb-json characters that are neither " nor \.
    source
    theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.escapeChar_passthrough_is_valid (c : Char) (h_not_escaped : Emit.escapeChar c = c.toString) :
    CharPredicates.isNbJsonBool c = true ∧ c ≠ '\"' ∧ c ≠ '\\'

    An unescaped character (one that escapeChar passes through as-is) is a valid nb-json character that is neither " nor \.

    source
    theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.escapeChar_output_nbJson (c ch : Char) :
    ch ∈ (Emit.escapeChar c).toList → CharPredicates.isNbJsonBool ch = true

    Every character of escapeChar c is a valid nb-json character. This is needed because collectDoubleQuotedLoop checks isNbJsonBool on each character it encounters.

    §2 Emitter Output Properties #

    Properties of the strings produced by emit that are needed for scanner acceptance.

    source
    theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.emit_nonempty (v : YamlValue) :
    (Emit.emit v).length > 0

    The output of emit v is non-empty for any value.

    §2.1 escapeString Decomposition #

    Helper lemmas for inductive reasoning about escapeString output. The key fact: escapeString is a monoid homomorphism that concatenates escapeChar c for each character c in the input.

    source
    theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.string_foldl_toList {α : Type u_1} (f : α → Char → α) (init : α) (s : String) :
    String.foldl f init s = List.foldl f init s.toList
    source
    theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.escapeString_foldl_shift (chars : List Char) (init : String) :
    List.foldl (fun (acc : String) (c : Char) => acc ++ Emit.escapeChar c) init chars = init ++ List.foldl (fun (acc : String) (c : Char) => acc ++ Emit.escapeChar c) "" chars

    The accumulator-shift property for escapeString's foldl: prepending to the accumulator is the same as prepending to the result.

    source
    theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.escapeString_nil :
    Emit.escapeString "" = ""

    escapeString on empty string.

    source
    theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.escapeString_cons (c : Char) (cs : List Char) :
    Emit.escapeString (String.ofList (c :: cs)) = Emit.escapeChar c ++ Emit.escapeString (String.ofList cs)

    escapeString distributes over cons: the output is the escape of the first character followed by the escape of the rest.

    §2.2 First-Character Properties #

    The first character of escapeChar c output determines which branch of collectDoubleQuotedLoop processes it.

    source
    theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.escapeChar_head_not_quote (c : Char) :
    (Emit.escapeChar c).toList.head? ≠ some '\"'

    The first character of escapeChar c is never ". This ensures the scanner never mistakes escape output for a closing quote.

    source
    theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.escapeChar_head_not_linebreak (c ch : Char) :
    (Emit.escapeChar c).toList.head? = some ch → CharPredicates.isLineBreakBool ch = false

    The first character of escapeChar c is never a line break. This ensures collectDoubleQuotedLoop never takes the newline-fold branch.

    source
    theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.escapeChar_output_no_linebreak (c ch : Char) :
    ch ∈ (Emit.escapeChar c).toList → CharPredicates.isLineBreakBool ch = false

    No character of escapeChar c is a line break. Stronger than escapeChar_head_not_linebreak — covers ALL output chars.

    source
    theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.escapeChar_nonempty (c : Char) :
    (Emit.escapeChar c).toList ≠ []

    The output of escapeChar c is non-empty.

    §2.3 escapeString Character Properties #

    Since escapeString s = s.foldl (fun acc c => acc ++ escapeChar c) "", every character of the output is a character of some escapeChar c. We lift per-character properties from escapeChar to escapeString.

    source
    theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.foldl_append_toList_eq_flatMap (chars : List Char) (f : Char → String) :
    (List.foldl (fun (acc : String) (c : Char) => acc ++ f c) "" chars).toList = List.flatMap (fun (c : Char) => (f c).toList) chars

    Generic: foldl with string append equals flatMap on character lists.

    source
    theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.escapeString_mem_iff (content : String) (ch : Char) :
    ch ∈ (Emit.escapeString content).toList ↔ ∃ (c : Char), c ∈ content.toList ∧ ch ∈ (Emit.escapeChar c).toList

    A character is in escapeString content iff it is in some escapeChar c for c in content.

    source
    theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.escapeString_all_nbJson (content : String) (ch : Char) :
    ch ∈ (Emit.escapeString content).toList → CharPredicates.isNbJsonBool ch = true

    All chars of escapeString content are valid nb-json characters.

    source
    theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.escapeString_no_linebreak (content : String) (ch : Char) :
    ch ∈ (Emit.escapeString content).toList → CharPredicates.isLineBreakBool ch = false

    No character of escapeString content is a line break.

    §2.4 collectDoubleQuotedLoop Acceptance of Escaped Strings #

    The core lemma: collectDoubleQuotedLoop succeeds on escapeString content ++ "\"" for any content string. This is the key step in proving scanner acceptance of canonical emitter output.

    The proof proceeds by induction on content.toList:

    • Base case: escapeString "" = "", remaining is ['"'] → close quote.
    • Inductive step: escapeString (c :: cs) = escapeChar c ++ escapeString cs. Three sub-cases for escapeChar c:
      • Passthrough (escapeChar c = c.toString): regular char branch, 1 fuel unit.
      • Named escape (escapeTag c = some tag): escape branch, processEscape consumes tag, 1 fuel unit.
      • Hex escape (c.val.toNat < 0x20, no named tag): escape branch, processEscape consumes \xHH, 1 fuel unit.
    source
    theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.peek_of_chars_cons (sc : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (rest : List Char) (col : Nat) (hcorr : CouplingBridge.ScannerSurfCorr sc { chars := c :: rest, col := col }) :
    sc.peek? = some c ∧ sc.offset < sc.inputEnd

    Derive peek? from ScannerSurfCorr when the surface position starts with a known character. Column can be arbitrary.

    source
    theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.processEscape_named_ok (sc : Scanner.ScannerState) (c tag : Char) (h_tag : RoundTrip.escapeTag c = some tag) (h_peek : sc.peek? = some tag) :
    ∃ (decoded : Char), Scanner.processEscape sc = Except.ok (decoded, sc.advance)

    processEscape succeeds when peeking at a named escape tag (one of escapeTag's output characters). Returns (decoded, sc.advance).

    source
    theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.processEscape_named_content (sc : Scanner.ScannerState) (c tag : Char) (h_tag : RoundTrip.escapeTag c = some tag) (h_peek : sc.peek? = some tag) :
    Scanner.processEscape sc = Except.ok (c, sc.advance)

    Strengthened named escape: processEscape on a named escape tag returns the ORIGINAL character c, not just some existential decoded value. This is the content-preservation key for the round-trip proof.

    source
    theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.escapeTag_not_linebreak (c tag : Char) (h_tag : RoundTrip.escapeTag c = some tag) :
    CharPredicates.isLineBreakBool tag = false

    Named escape tags are never line breaks — needed to distinguish escape from escaped-newline in the scanner.

    source
    theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.escapeChar_passthrough_toList (c : Char) (h : RoundTrip.isEscapedChar c = false) :
    (Emit.escapeChar c).toList = [c]

    escapeChar for passthrough characters produces a single-element char list equal to [c].

    source
    theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.escapeChar_named_toList (c tag : Char) (h : RoundTrip.escapeTag c = some tag) :
    (Emit.escapeChar c).toList = ['\\', tag]

    escapeChar for named escapes produces exactly ['\\', tag].

    source
    def L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scannerHexCheck (c : Char) :
    Bool

    Scanner's hex digit check, matching collectHexDigitsLoop's condition.

    Equations
    • L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scannerHexCheck c = (c.isDigit || decide (c ≥ 'a') && decide (c ≤ 'f') || decide (c ≥ 'A') && decide (c ≤ 'F'))
    Instances For
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.hexNibble_is_hex (n : Fin 16) :
      scannerHexCheck (Emit.hexNibble ↑n) = true
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.hexNibble_lt128 (n : Fin 16) :
      (Emit.hexNibble ↑n).toNat < 128
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.hex_two_foldl_bound (n1 n2 : Fin 128) :
      scannerHexCheck (Char.ofNat ↑n1) = true → scannerHexCheck (Char.ofNat ↑n2) = true → String.foldl (fun (acc : Nat) (c : Char) => acc * 16 + if c.isDigit = true then c.toNat - '0'.toNat else if c ≥ 'a' then c.toNat - 'a'.toNat + 10 else c.toNat - 'A'.toNat + 10) 0 (("".push (Char.ofNat ↑n1)).push (Char.ofNat ↑n2)) < 1114112

      For any two hex chars (each with toNat < 128 and scannerHexCheck = true), the 2-digit hex foldl value is < 0x110000.

      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.escapeChar_hex_structure (c : Char) (h_lt : c.val.toNat < 32) (h_no_tag : RoundTrip.escapeTag c = none) :
      ∃ (h1 : Char), ∃ (h2 : Char), (Emit.escapeChar c).toList = ['\\', 'x', h1, h2] ∧ h1 ≠ '\n' ∧ h1 ≠ '\x0d' ∧ h2 ≠ '\n' ∧ h2 ≠ '\x0d' ∧ scannerHexCheck h1 = true ∧ scannerHexCheck h2 = true ∧ h1.toNat < 128 ∧ h2.toNat < 128

      escapeChar output for hex-escaped characters (C0 controls with no named tag) has the form ['\\', 'x', h1, h2] where h1, h2 are hex digits with toNat < 128.

      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.advance_line_of_peek (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (h_lt : s.offset < s.inputEnd) (h_peek : s.peek? = some c) (hnl : c ≠ '\n') (hcr : c ≠ '\x0d') :
      s.advance.line = s.line
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.processEscape_hex_ok (sc : Scanner.ScannerState) (h1 h2 : Char) (rest : List Char) (col : Nat) (hcorr : CouplingBridge.ScannerSurfCorr sc { chars := 'x' :: h1 :: h2 :: rest, col := col }) (h_h1_nn : h1 ≠ '\n') (h_h1_cr : h1 ≠ '\x0d') (h_h2_nn : h2 ≠ '\n') (h_h2_cr : h2 ≠ '\x0d') (h_h1_hex : scannerHexCheck h1 = true) (h_h2_hex : scannerHexCheck h2 = true) (h_h1_lt128 : h1.toNat < 128) (h_h2_lt128 : h2.toNat < 128) :
      ∃ (decoded : Char), ∃ (s' : Scanner.ScannerState), Scanner.processEscape sc = Except.ok (decoded, s') ∧ CouplingBridge.ScannerSurfCorr s' { chars := rest, col := col + 3 } ∧ s'.inputEnd = sc.inputEnd ∧ s'.line = sc.line

      processEscape succeeds on hex escape sequences produced by escapeHex2. When the scanner is positioned at 'x' :: h1 :: h2 :: rest, processEscape reads x, then parseHexEscape consumes h1 h2.

      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.push_append_ofList_eq (s : String) (c : Char) (cs : List Char) :
      s.push c ++ String.ofList cs = s ++ String.ofList (c :: cs)
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.append_ofList_nil (s : String) :
      s ++ String.ofList [] = s
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.hex_foldl_roundtrip (n : Fin 32) :
      have h1 := Emit.hexNibble (↑n / 16); have h2 := Emit.hexNibble (↑n % 16); String.foldl (fun (acc : Nat) (c : Char) => acc * 16 + if c.isDigit = true then c.toNat - '0'.toNat else if c ≥ 'a' then c.toNat - 'a'.toNat + 10 else c.toNat - 'A'.toNat + 10) 0 (("".push h1).push h2) = ↑n
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.collectDoubleQuotedLoop_escapeString_succeeds (sc : Scanner.ScannerState) (content_rest rest : List Char) (acc : String) (fuel : Nat) (startPos : YamlPos) (inFlow : Bool) (currentIndent : Int) (hcorr : CouplingBridge.ScannerSurfCorr sc { chars := (Emit.escapeString (String.ofList content_rest)).toList ++ ['\"'] ++ rest, col := sc.col }) (h_fuel : fuel ≥ content_rest.length + 1) :
      ∃ (s' : Scanner.ScannerState), Scanner.collectDoubleQuotedLoop sc acc fuel startPos inFlow currentIndent sc.inputEnd = Except.ok (acc ++ String.ofList content_rest, s') ∧ CouplingBridge.ScannerSurfCorr s' { chars := rest, col := s'.col } ∧ s'.col > 0 ∧ s'.line = sc.line

      The core loop lemma: collectDoubleQuotedLoop succeeds on escapeString content ++ "\"" ++ rest for any content string, leaving the scanner with ScannerSurfCorr at rest.

      By induction on the characters of content:

      • Base: closing " → loop terminates.
      • Passthrough char: regular char branch → recurse.
      • Named escape: \tag → processEscape → recurse.
      • Hex escape: \xHH → processEscape → recurse.

      §3 Scanner Acceptance of Canonical Output (Step 1) #

      The main technical content: proving the scanner accepts emitter output.

      §3.0 Helpers for scanner dispatch on emitter output #

      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.CharsFromOffset_length_le {input : String} {offset : Nat} {chars : List Char} (h : CouplingBridge.CharsFromOffset input offset chars) :
      chars.length ≤ input.utf8ByteSize - offset
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.escapeChar_toList_length_pos (c : Char) :
      (Emit.escapeChar c).toList.length ≥ 1
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.escapeString_length_ge (cs : List Char) :
      (Emit.escapeString (String.ofList cs)).toList.length ≥ cs.length
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.validateTrailingContent_peek_none (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (inputEnd : Nat) (h_peek : s.peek? = none) :
      Scanner.validateTrailingContent s inputEnd = Except.ok ()
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanDoubleQuoted_emitScalar_ok (sc : Scanner.ScannerState) (content : String) (hcorr : CouplingBridge.ScannerSurfCorr sc { chars := ['\"'] ++ (Emit.escapeString content).toList ++ ['\"'], col := sc.col }) (h_not_flow : sc.inFlow = false) :
      ∃ (s' : Scanner.ScannerState), Scanner.scanDoubleQuoted sc = Except.ok s' ∧ s'.peek? = none ∧ s'.tokens = sc.tokens.push { pos := sc.currentPos, val := YamlToken.scalar content ScalarStyle.doubleQuoted }
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.peek_none_of_empty_surf (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (col : Nat) (hcorr : CouplingBridge.ScannerSurfCorr s { chars := [], col := col }) :
      s.peek? = none

      If the surface position has empty remaining chars, then peek? = none.

      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanNextToken_eof (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (h_peek : s.peek? = none) :
      Scanner.scanNextToken s = Except.ok none
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.dispatchContent_quote (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (hc : c = '\"') (h_notFlow : s.flowLevel = 0) (h_indent : s.currentIndent = -1) (h_noDocStart : Scanner.atDocumentStart s = false) (h_noDocEnd : Scanner.atDocumentEnd s = false) :
      Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchStructural s c = Except.ok none ∧ Scanner.scanNextToken_checkBlockFlowIndent s c = Except.ok () ∧ Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchFlowIndicators s c = Except.ok none ∧ Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchBlockIndicators s c = Except.ok none
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.ScannerSurfCorr_transfer {sc sc' : Scanner.ScannerState} {sp : Surface.SurfPos} (hcorr : CouplingBridge.ScannerSurfCorr sc sp) (h_input : sc'.input = sc.input) (h_offset : sc'.offset = sc.offset) (h_inputEnd : sc'.inputEnd = sc.inputEnd) (h_col : sc'.col = sc.col) (h_indents : sc'.indents = sc.indents) :
      CouplingBridge.ScannerSurfCorr sc' sp
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.emitScalar_toList (content : String) :
      (Emit.emitScalar content).toList = ['\"'] ++ (Emit.escapeString content).toList ++ ['\"']
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.emitScalar_utf8ByteSize_ge (content : String) :
      (Emit.emitScalar content).utf8ByteSize ≥ 2
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanLoop_two_iter {s₀ s₁ : Scanner.ScannerState} {fuel : Nat} (h_fuel : fuel ≥ 2) (h_snt0 : Scanner.scanNextToken s₀ = Except.ok (some s₁)) (h_snt1 : Scanner.scanNextToken s₁ = Except.ok none) (h_flow : s₁.flowLevel = 0) (h_dp : s₁.directivesPresent = false) :
      ∃ (toks : Array (Positioned YamlToken)), Scanner.scanLoop s₀ fuel = Except.ok toks
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanLoop_two_iter_eq {s₀ s₁ : Scanner.ScannerState} {fuel : Nat} (h_fuel : fuel ≥ 2) (h_snt0 : Scanner.scanNextToken s₀ = Except.ok (some s₁)) (h_snt1 : Scanner.scanNextToken s₁ = Except.ok none) (h_flow : s₁.flowLevel = 0) (h_dp : s₁.directivesPresent = false) :
      Scanner.scanLoop s₀ fuel = Except.ok ((Scanner.unwindIndents s₁ (-1)).emit YamlToken.streamEnd).tokens
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanLoop_step_eq {s₀ s₁ : Scanner.ScannerState} {fuel : Nat} {toks : Array (Positioned YamlToken)} (h_snt : Scanner.scanNextToken s₀ = Except.ok (some s₁)) (h_loop : Scanner.scanLoop s₁ fuel = Except.ok toks) :
      Scanner.scanLoop s₀ (fuel + 1) = Except.ok toks

      Forward step: If scanNextToken produces a new state s₁, and scanLoop s₁ fuel succeeds, then scanLoop s₀ (fuel + 1) succeeds with the same result.

      This is the key compositionality lemma for scanner acceptance proofs: compose N steps backwards from scanLoop_two_iter (or scanLoop_eof) using repeated applications of scanLoop_step_eq.

      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanLoop_step {s₀ s₁ : Scanner.ScannerState} {fuel : Nat} (h_snt : Scanner.scanNextToken s₀ = Except.ok (some s₁)) (h_loop : ∃ (toks : Array (Positioned YamlToken)), Scanner.scanLoop s₁ fuel = Except.ok toks) :
      ∃ (toks : Array (Positioned YamlToken)), Scanner.scanLoop s₀ (fuel + 1) = Except.ok toks

      Existential version of scanLoop_step_eq.

      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanLoop_fuel_mono {s : Scanner.ScannerState} {fuel₁ fuel₂ : Nat} {toks : Array (Positioned YamlToken)} (h : Scanner.scanLoop s fuel₁ = Except.ok toks) (h_le : fuel₁ ≤ fuel₂) :
      Scanner.scanLoop s fuel₂ = Except.ok toks

      Fuel monotonicity: If scanLoop succeeds with fuel₁, it succeeds with any larger fuel fuel₂ ≥ fuel₁, producing the same token array.

      Proof by induction on fuel₁. Each scanLoop iteration either terminates (EOF/error → fuel irrelevant) or recurses with one less fuel (→ inductive hypothesis).

      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanLoop_eof {s : Scanner.ScannerState} (h_snt : Scanner.scanNextToken s = Except.ok none) (h_fl : s.flowLevel = 0) (h_dp : s.directivesPresent = false) :
      ∃ (toks : Array (Positioned YamlToken)), Scanner.scanLoop s 1 = Except.ok toks

      Terminal step: If scanNextToken returns .ok none (EOF), scanLoop with fuel ≥ 1 terminates successfully.

      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanLoop_eof_eq {s : Scanner.ScannerState} {fuel : Nat} (h_fuel : fuel ≥ 1) (h_snt : Scanner.scanNextToken s = Except.ok none) (h_fl : s.flowLevel = 0) (h_dp : s.directivesPresent = false) :
      Scanner.scanLoop s fuel = Except.ok ((Scanner.unwindIndents s (-1)).emit YamlToken.streamEnd).tokens

      Terminal step (equality): If scanNextToken returns .ok none (EOF), scanLoop produces exactly the unwind+streamEnd tokens.

      source
      inductive L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.ScanChain :
      Scanner.ScannerState → Nat → Scanner.ScannerState → Prop

      ScanChain s n s' means n successive scanNextToken calls starting from s each return .ok (some ...), with the final state being s'. Used to express that the scanner processes a multi-token sub-expression (e.g., emit v within a flow collection).

      • zero {s : Scanner.ScannerState} : ScanChain s 0 s
      • step {s s_mid s' : Scanner.ScannerState} {n : Nat} : Scanner.scanNextToken s = Except.ok (some s_mid) → ScanChain s_mid n s' → ScanChain s (n + 1) s'
      Instances For
        source
        theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.ScanChain.trans {s₁ s₂ s₃ : Scanner.ScannerState} {n₁ n₂ : Nat} (h1 : ScanChain s₁ n₁ s₂) (h2 : ScanChain s₂ n₂ s₃) :
        ScanChain s₁ (n₁ + n₂) s₃

        Transitivity: concatenate two scan chains.

        source
        theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.ScanChain.single {s s' : Scanner.ScannerState} (h : Scanner.scanNextToken s = Except.ok (some s')) :
        ScanChain s 1 s'

        A single scanNextToken step as a ScanChain.

        source
        theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.ScanChain.to_scanLoop {s s' : Scanner.ScannerState} {n fuel : Nat} {toks : Array (Positioned YamlToken)} (h_chain : ScanChain s n s') (h_loop : Scanner.scanLoop s' fuel = Except.ok toks) :
        Scanner.scanLoop s (fuel + n) = Except.ok toks

        Connect a ScanChain to scanLoop: if N steps succeed reaching s', and scanLoop s' fuel succeeds, then scanLoop s (fuel + N) succeeds with the same result.

        source
        theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.ScanChain.to_scanLoop_exists {s s' : Scanner.ScannerState} {n : Nat} (h_chain : ScanChain s n s') (h_loop : ∃ (fuel : Nat), ∃ (toks : Array (Positioned YamlToken)), Scanner.scanLoop s' fuel = Except.ok toks) :
        ∃ (fuel : Nat), ∃ (toks : Array (Positioned YamlToken)), Scanner.scanLoop s fuel = Except.ok toks

        Connect a ScanChain to scanLoop (existential version).

        source
        theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanNextToken_preserves_bound (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h : Scanner.scanNextToken s = Except.ok (some s')) (h_le : s.offset ≤ s.inputEnd) (h_ie : s.inputEnd = s.input.utf8ByteSize) (h_iv : String.Pos.Raw.IsValid s.input { byteIdx := s.offset }) :
        s'.offset ≤ s'.inputEnd ∧ s'.inputEnd = s.inputEnd ∧ s'.input = s.input ∧ String.Pos.Raw.IsValid s'.input { byteIdx := s'.offset }

        The chain fuel bound: any ScanChain followed by EOF fits within the standard fuel (input.utf8ByteSize + 1) * 4.

        Proof strategy:

        1. scanNextToken_progress → each step advances offset by ≥ 1
        2. By induction: s_final.offset ≥ s₀.offset + n
        3. s₀.offset = 0 (from mk' + emit streamStart)
        4. s_final.offset ≤ s_final.inputEnd (from upper bound preservation)
        5. s_final.inputEnd = input.utf8ByteSize (from inputEnd preservation)
        6. Combining: n ≤ utf8ByteSize ≤ (utf8ByteSize + 1) * 4
        source
        theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.ScanChain.bound_invariant {s₀ s_final : Scanner.ScannerState} {n : Nat} (h_chain : ScanChain s₀ n s_final) (h_le : s₀.offset ≤ s₀.inputEnd) (h_ie : s₀.inputEnd = s₀.input.utf8ByteSize) (h_iv : String.Pos.Raw.IsValid s₀.input { byteIdx := s₀.offset }) :
        s_final.offset ≥ s₀.offset + n ∧ s_final.offset ≤ s_final.inputEnd ∧ s_final.inputEnd = s₀.inputEnd
        source
        theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.ScanChain.fuel_bound (input : String) (s₀ s_final : Scanner.ScannerState) (n : Nat) (h_s0 : s₀ = (Scanner.ScannerState.mk' input).emit YamlToken.streamStart) (h_chain : ScanChain s₀ n s_final) (_h_eof : Scanner.scanNextToken s_final = Except.ok none) :
        n + 1 ≤ (input.utf8ByteSize + 1) * 4
        source
        inductive L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.FlowMonoChain (fl₀ : Nat) :
        Scanner.ScannerState → Nat → Scanner.ScannerState → Prop

        FlowMonoChain fl₀ s n s' is a ScanChain where every intermediate state has flowLevel ≥ fl₀. This captures the "flow-balanced" property: the chain never closes brackets below the initial flow depth, ensuring stacked simple keys from before the chain are never restored.

        Motivation: ScanChain_filtered_prefix needs to show that setIfInBounds (from scanValuePrepare) never writes at token positions below the initial range. This holds when the simpleKeyStack is never popped below its initial height, which follows from flowLevel ≥ fl₀ at every step (since simpleKeyStack.size tracks flowLevel via scanFlowStart/scanFlowEnd push/pop synchronization).

        For emitter-produced chains, fl₀ = s.flowLevel is always satisfied because the emitter produces balanced bracket sequences: every ]/} matches an inner [/{.

        • zero {fl₀ : Nat} {s : Scanner.ScannerState} (h_fl : s.flowLevel ≥ fl₀) : FlowMonoChain fl₀ s 0 s
        • step {fl₀ : Nat} {s s_mid s' : Scanner.ScannerState} {n : Nat} (h_fl : s.flowLevel ≥ fl₀) (h_snt : Scanner.scanNextToken s = Except.ok (some s_mid)) (h_rest : FlowMonoChain fl₀ s_mid n s') : FlowMonoChain fl₀ s (n + 1) s'
        Instances For
          source
          theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.FlowMonoChain.toScanChain {fl₀ : Nat} {s s' : Scanner.ScannerState} {n : Nat} (h : FlowMonoChain fl₀ s n s') :
          ScanChain s n s'

          Degrade a FlowMonoChain to a plain ScanChain by forgetting flow-level bounds.

          source
          theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.FlowMonoChain.flowLevel_ge_start {fl₀ : Nat} {s s' : Scanner.ScannerState} {n : Nat} (h : FlowMonoChain fl₀ s n s') :
          s.flowLevel ≥ fl₀

          The start state of a FlowMonoChain has flowLevel ≥ fl₀.

          source
          theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.FlowMonoChain.flowLevel_ge_end {fl₀ : Nat} {s s' : Scanner.ScannerState} {n : Nat} (h : FlowMonoChain fl₀ s n s') :
          s'.flowLevel ≥ fl₀

          The end state of a FlowMonoChain has flowLevel ≥ fl₀.

          source
          theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.FlowMonoChain.single {fl₀ : Nat} {s s' : Scanner.ScannerState} (h_snt : Scanner.scanNextToken s = Except.ok (some s')) (h_fl : s.flowLevel ≥ fl₀) (h_fl' : s'.flowLevel ≥ fl₀) :
          FlowMonoChain fl₀ s 1 s'

          A single scanNextToken step as a FlowMonoChain.

          source
          theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.FlowMonoChain.trans {fl₀ : Nat} {s₁ s₂ s₃ : Scanner.ScannerState} {n₁ n₂ : Nat} (h1 : FlowMonoChain fl₀ s₁ n₁ s₂) (h2 : FlowMonoChain fl₀ s₂ n₂ s₃) :
          FlowMonoChain fl₀ s₁ (n₁ + n₂) s₃

          Transitivity: concatenate two FlowMonoChains with the same floor.

          source
          theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.FlowMonoChain.weaken {fl₀ fl₁ : Nat} {s s' : Scanner.ScannerState} {n : Nat} (h : FlowMonoChain fl₁ s n s') (h_le : fl₀ ≤ fl₁) :
          FlowMonoChain fl₀ s n s'

          Weaken the flow-level floor: if fl₀ ≤ fl₁, a FlowMonoChain fl₁ is also a FlowMonoChain fl₀.

          source
          theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.FlowMonoChain.tokens_mono {fl₀ : Nat} {s s' : Scanner.ScannerState} {n : Nat} (h : FlowMonoChain fl₀ s n s') :
          s'.tokens.size ≥ s.tokens.size

          Token monotonicity for FlowMonoChain: tokens only grow through the chain (delegates to ScanChain version).

          SimpleKeyAboveFloor: flow-level-aware simple key invariant #

          The SimpleKeyAboveFloor predicate is like SimpleKeyAbove but only constrains stack entries at index ≥ stackFloor, with a size guarantee. It is designed for use with FlowMonoChain where the stack floor equals the initial flow level.

          source
          def L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.SimpleKeyAboveFloor (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (n stackFloor : Nat) :
          Prop
          Equations
          • One or more equations did not get rendered due to their size.

            SimpleKeyAboveFloor s_out n fl₀

            SimpleKeyAboveFloor s_out n fl₀

            SimpleKeyAboveFloor s_out n fl₀

            SimpleKeyAboveFloor s_out n fl₀
            source
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.SimpleKeyAboveFloor_of_flow_close (s_out s_in : Scanner.ScannerState) (n fl₀ : Nat) (h_sk : s_out.simpleKey = s_in.simpleKeyStack.back?.getD { }) (h_stack : s_out.simpleKeyStack = s_in.simpleKeyStack.pop) (h_inv : SimpleKeyAboveFloor s_in n fl₀) (h_size : s_in.simpleKeyStack.size > fl₀ ∨ fl₀ = 0) :

            SimpleKeyAboveFloor s' n₀ fl₀

            FlowMonoChain prefix preservation (Step 4) #

            Token prefix preservation through a FlowMonoChain, using SimpleKeyAboveFloor instead of SimpleKeyAbove. The key insight is that scanNextToken_preserves_prefix only reads the simpleKey conjunct (not the stack entries), so we can replicate its proof using just SimpleKeyAboveFloor.1.

            source
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanNextToken_preserves_prefix_of_skFloor (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h_next : Scanner.scanNextToken s = Except.ok (some s')) (n : Nat) (h_n : n ≤ s.tokens.size) (h_sk : s.simpleKey.possible = true → s.simpleKey.tokenIndex ≥ n) (i : Nat) (h_bound : i < n) :
            s'.tokens[i] = s.tokens[i]
            source

            (∀ (i : Nat) (hi : i < n₀), s'.tokens[i] = s.tokens[i]) ∧ SimpleKeyAboveFloor s' n₀ fl₀

            s'.simpleKeyStack.size ≥ s'.flowLevel

            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanNextToken_preserves_sync (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h_next : Scanner.scanNextToken s = Except.ok (some s')) (h_sync : s.simpleKeyStack.size ≥ s.flowLevel) :
            s'.simpleKeyStack.size ≥ s'.flowLevel
            source
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.FlowMonoChain_preserves_raw_prefix {s s' : Scanner.ScannerState} {n fl₀ : Nat} (h_fmc : FlowMonoChain fl₀ s n s') (n₀ : Nat) (h_n₀ : n₀ ≤ s.tokens.size) (h_stack_floor : SimpleKeyAboveFloor s n₀ fl₀) (h_sync : s.simpleKeyStack.size ≥ s.flowLevel) (i : Nat) (hi : i < n₀) :
            s'.tokens[i] = s.tokens[i]
            source
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanFiltered_of_chain (input : String) (s₀ s_final : Scanner.ScannerState) (n : Nat) (h_s0 : s₀ = (Scanner.ScannerState.mk' input).emit YamlToken.streamStart) (h_no_bom : (Scanner.ScannerState.mk' input).peek? ≠ some '﻿') (h_chain : ScanChain s₀ n s_final) (h_eof : Scanner.scanNextToken s_final = Except.ok none) (h_fl : s_final.flowLevel = 0) (h_dp : s_final.directivesPresent = false) (h_fuel : n + 1 ≤ (input.utf8ByteSize + 1) * 4) :
            ∃ (tokens : Array (Positioned YamlToken)), Scanner.scanFiltered input = Except.ok tokens

            Connect a ScanChain to scanFiltered: if N steps succeed reaching a state where scanNextToken returns none (EOF), then scanFiltered on the input succeeds. Requires flowLevel = 0 and directivesPresent = false at the end.

            source
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanFiltered_of_chain_eq (input : String) (s₀ s_final : Scanner.ScannerState) (n : Nat) (h_s0 : s₀ = (Scanner.ScannerState.mk' input).emit YamlToken.streamStart) (h_no_bom : (Scanner.ScannerState.mk' input).peek? ≠ some '﻿') (h_chain : ScanChain s₀ n s_final) (h_eof : Scanner.scanNextToken s_final = Except.ok none) (h_fl : s_final.flowLevel = 0) (h_dp : s_final.directivesPresent = false) (h_fuel : n + 1 ≤ (input.utf8ByteSize + 1) * 4) :
            Scanner.scanFiltered input = Except.ok (Array.filter (fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder) ((Scanner.unwindIndents s_final (-1)).emit YamlToken.streamEnd).tokens)

            Equality version: gives the exact filtered token array from a ScanChain. The output is the filtered version of the chain's final state tokens plus streamEnd, after unwinding indents.

            source
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanNextToken_eq_of_preprocess (s₁ s₂ : Scanner.ScannerState) (h : Scanner.scanNextToken_preprocess s₁ = Scanner.scanNextToken_preprocess s₂) :
            Scanner.scanNextToken s₁ = Scanner.scanNextToken s₂
            source
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.ScanChain_of_scanNextToken_eq {s₁ s₂ s' : Scanner.ScannerState} {n : Nat} (h_eq : Scanner.scanNextToken s₁ = Scanner.scanNextToken s₂) (h_chain : ScanChain s₂ (n + 1) s') :
            ScanChain s₁ (n + 1) s'
            source
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.FlowMonoChain_of_scanNextToken_eq {fl₀ : Nat} {s₁ s₂ s' : Scanner.ScannerState} {n : Nat} (h_eq : Scanner.scanNextToken s₁ = Scanner.scanNextToken s₂) (h_fl : s₁.flowLevel ≥ fl₀) (h_chain : FlowMonoChain fl₀ s₂ (n + 1) s') :
            FlowMonoChain fl₀ s₁ (n + 1) s'

            FlowMonoChain version of ScanChain_of_scanNextToken_eq: if scanNextToken gives the same result for two states, and the second has a FlowMonoChain of length ≥ 1, then the first does too (given the flow-level bound at the first state).

            source
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanNextToken_via_flow_dispatch (s s_pp s_ad s_result : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (h_pp : Scanner.scanNextToken_preprocess s = Except.ok (some (s_pp, c))) (h_struct : Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchStructural s_pp c = Except.ok none) (h_ad_eq : s_ad = if s_pp.allowDirectives = true then { input := s_pp.input, inputEnd := s_pp.inputEnd, offset := s_pp.offset, line := s_pp.line, col := s_pp.col, indents := s_pp.indents, flowLevel := s_pp.flowLevel, tokens := s_pp.tokens, simpleKey := s_pp.simpleKey, simpleKeyStack := s_pp.simpleKeyStack, simpleKeyAllowed := s_pp.simpleKeyAllowed, needIndentCheck := s_pp.needIndentCheck, allowDirectives := false, seenYamlDirective := s_pp.seenYamlDirective, directivesPresent := s_pp.directivesPresent, documentEverStarted := true, flowStack := s_pp.flowStack, explicitKeyLine := s_pp.explicitKeyLine, comments := s_pp.comments, definedAnchors := s_pp.definedAnchors } else s_pp) (h_check : Scanner.scanNextToken_checkBlockFlowIndent s_ad c = Except.ok ()) (h_flow : Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchFlowIndicators s_ad c = Except.ok (some s_result)) :
            Scanner.scanNextToken s = Except.ok (some s_result)

            When preprocessing succeeds, structural dispatch returns none, and flow indicator dispatch produces a result, then scanNextToken returns that result. This captures the common case for flow indicator characters [[, ], {, }, ,].

            s_ad is the state after the allowDirectives update, defined as: if s_pp.allowDirectives then { s_pp with allowDirectives := false, documentEverStarted := true } else s_pp

            Scanner.lastRealTokenVal? (tokens.push tok) = some tok.val

            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanDoubleQuoted_flow_ok (sc : Scanner.ScannerState) (content : String) (rest : List Char) (hcorr : CouplingBridge.ScannerSurfCorr sc { chars := ['\"'] ++ (Emit.escapeString content).toList ++ ['\"'] ++ rest, col := sc.col }) (h_flow : sc.inFlow = true) :
            ∃ (s' : Scanner.ScannerState), Scanner.scanDoubleQuoted sc = Except.ok s' ∧ CouplingBridge.ScannerSurfCorr s' { chars := rest, col := s'.col } ∧ s'.flowLevel = sc.flowLevel ∧ s'.directivesPresent = sc.directivesPresent ∧ s'.indents = sc.indents ∧ s'.explicitKeyLine = sc.explicitKeyLine ∧ s'.col > 0 ∧ Scanner.lastRealTokenVal? s'.tokens = some (YamlToken.scalar content ScalarStyle.doubleQuoted) ∧ s'.simpleKeyAllowed = false ∧ s'.line = sc.line
            source

            Scanner.skipWhitespace s = s
            source
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.skipSpaces_of_not_space (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (h_pk : s.peek? = some c) (h_ns : c ≠ ' ') :
            Scanner.skipSpaces s = s

            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.skipToContent_of_content_char (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (h_pk : s.peek? = some c) (h_nws : CharPredicates.isWhiteSpaceBool c = false) (h_nlb : CharPredicates.isLineBreakBool c = false) (h_nc : c ≠ '#') (h_more : s.offset < s.inputEnd) :
            Scanner.skipToContent s = Except.ok s

            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanValueValidate_ok_of_not_possible_ek_none (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (h_ek : s.explicitKeyLine = none) (h_sk : s.simpleKey.possible = false) :
