                                Infrastructure for filtered token tracking (Sub-phase 4.4.G) #

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.unwindIndents_noop_short_stack (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (h_stack : s.indents.size ≤ 1) :
                                Scanner.unwindIndents s (-1) = s

                                unwindIndents is identity when the indent stack has at most 1 entry. This covers emitter output where indents = #[] (the default from ScannerState.mk'). unwindIndentsLoop checks s.indents.size > 1 before unwinding; with size ≤ 1, the condition fails immediately and the state is returned unchanged.

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.emit_tokens_push (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (tok : YamlToken) :
                                (s.emit tok).tokens = s.tokens.push { pos := s.currentPos, val := tok }

                                After emit streamEnd, the pushed token is the last element of the array. Gives: (s.emit tok).tokens = s.tokens.push ⟨s.currentPos, tok⟩.

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanFiltered_tokens_eq_of_chain_short_stack (input : String) (s₀ s_final : Scanner.ScannerState) (n : Nat) (h_s0 : s₀ = (Scanner.ScannerState.mk' input).emit YamlToken.streamStart) (h_no_bom : (Scanner.ScannerState.mk' input).peek? ≠ some '﻿') (h_chain : ScanChain s₀ n s_final) (h_eof : Scanner.scanNextToken s_final = Except.ok none) (h_fl : s_final.flowLevel = 0) (h_dp : s_final.directivesPresent = false) (h_fuel : n + 1 ≤ (input.utf8ByteSize + 1) * 4) (h_stack : s_final.indents.size ≤ 1) :
                                Scanner.scanFiltered input = Except.ok (Array.filter (fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder) (s_final.emit YamlToken.streamEnd).tokens)

                                When a ScanChain starts from s₀ via scanFiltered, the token array equation. Combines scanFiltered_of_chain_eq with unwindIndents identity for emitter states.

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.ScanChain_tokens_mono {s s' : Scanner.ScannerState} {n : Nat} (h_chain : ScanChain s n s') :
                                s'.tokens.size ≥ s.tokens.size

                                ScanChain token array monotonicity: tokens array size grows (non-strictly) through any scan chain.

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanNextToken_prefix_and_sk_inv (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h_next : Scanner.scanNextToken s = Except.ok (some s')) (n : Nat) (h_n : n ≤ s.tokens.size) (h_inv : ScannerCorrectness.SimpleKeyAbove s n) :
                                (∀ (i : Nat) (hi : i < n), s'.tokens[i] = s.tokens[i]) ∧ ScannerCorrectness.SimpleKeyAbove s' n

                                Combined per-step prefix preservation and simpleKey invariant maintenance.

                                Precondition: n ≤ s.tokens.size and the simpleKey condition s.simpleKey.possible → s.simpleKey.tokenIndex ≥ n, which says that the prefix index doesn't overlap the simpleKey placeholder position. Without this, scanNextToken may overwrite tokens[tokenIndex] (replacing .placeholder with .key), violating prefix preservation.

                                Precondition: Uses SimpleKeyAbove to track both the current simpleKey and all stacked simpleKeys. This is necessary because flow close operations (]/}) restore a simpleKey from the stack, and without stack bounds, the restored tokenIndex could fall below n.

                                Conclusion: Returns both prefix preservation and SimpleKeyAbove s' n, enabling straightforward induction in ScanChain_preserves_raw_prefix.

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.ScanChain_preserves_raw_prefix {s s' : Scanner.ScannerState} {k : Nat} (h_chain : ScanChain s k s') (n₀ : Nat) (h_n₀ : n₀ ≤ s.tokens.size) (h_inv : ScannerCorrectness.SimpleKeyAbove s n₀) (i : Nat) (hi : i < n₀) :
                                s'.tokens[i] = s.tokens[i]

                                Through a ScanChain, all raw token positions below n₀ are preserved, provided n₀ ≤ s.tokens.size and SimpleKeyAbove s n₀ holds (tracking both the current simpleKey and all stacked simpleKeys).

                                The SimpleKeyAbove invariant is maintained through each step by scanNextToken_prefix_and_sk_inv, making the induction straightforward.

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.Array_filter_prefix_of_raw_prefix {α : Type} (a b : Array α) (p : α → Bool) (h_sz : a.size ≤ b.size) (h_eq : ∀ (i : Nat) (hi : i < a.size), b[i] = a[i]) :
                                ∃ (suffix : List α), (Array.filter p b).toList = (Array.filter p a).toList ++ suffix

                                If b extends a (same elements at all positions i < a.size), then b.filter p has a.filter p as a prefix.

                                Filtered token array growth infrastructure #

                                Every scanNextToken step adds at least one non-placeholder token. The proof decomposes into:

                                1. Preprocessing (skipToContent, unwindIndents, saveSimpleKey) preserves or grows filtered count — from existing preprocess_preserves_prefix.
                                2. Each dispatch branch emits at least one non-placeholder token.
                                3. setIfInBounds with non-placeholder replacement doesn't decrease filtered count (only used in scanValuePrepare).
                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.List_filter_set_length_mono {α : Type} (l : List α) (i : Nat) (v : α) (p : α → Bool) (hv : p v = true) :
                                (List.filter p (l.set i v)).length ≥ (List.filter p l).length
                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.Array_setIfInBounds_filter_mono {α : Type} (a : Array α) (i : Nat) (v : α) (p : α → Bool) (hv : p v = true) :
                                (Array.filter p (a.setIfInBounds i v)).size ≥ (Array.filter p a).size
                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.preprocess_filtered_mono (s s₁ : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (h : Scanner.scanNextToken_preprocess s = Except.ok (some (s₁, c))) :
                                (Array.filter (fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder) s₁.tokens).size ≥ (Array.filter (fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder) s.tokens).size
                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.allowDir_ite_filter (s : Scanner.ScannerState) :
                                have p := fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder; (Array.filter p (if s.allowDirectives = true then { input := s.input, inputEnd := s.inputEnd, offset := s.offset, line := s.line, col := s.col, indents := s.indents, flowLevel := s.flowLevel, tokens := s.tokens, simpleKey := s.simpleKey, simpleKeyStack := s.simpleKeyStack, simpleKeyAllowed := s.simpleKeyAllowed, needIndentCheck := s.needIndentCheck, allowDirectives := false, seenYamlDirective := s.seenYamlDirective, directivesPresent := s.directivesPresent, documentEverStarted := true, flowStack := s.flowStack, explicitKeyLine := s.explicitKeyLine, comments := s.comments, definedAnchors := s.definedAnchors } else s).tokens).size = (Array.filter p s.tokens).size

                                General filtered growth helper #

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.List_filter_length_ge_one {α : Type} (l : List α) (p : α → Bool) (h_len : l.length ≥ 1) (h_head : p l[0] = true) :
                                (List.filter p l).length ≥ 1
                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.filtered_grows_of_extended_prefix {α : Type} (a b : Array α) (p : α → Bool) (h_sz : b.size ≥ a.size + 1) (h_pres : ∀ (i : Nat) (hi : i < a.size), b[i] = a[i]) (h_new : p b[a.size] = true) :
                                (Array.filter p b).size ≥ (Array.filter p a).size + 1
                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.filtered_grows_of_any_new {α : Type} (a b : Array α) (p : α → Bool) (h_sz : b.size ≥ a.size + 1) (h_pres : ∀ (i : Nat) (hi : i < a.size), b[i] = a[i]) (j : Nat) (hj_lo : a.size ≤ j) (hj_hi : j < b.size) (h_new : p b[j] = true) :
                                (Array.filter p b).size ≥ (Array.filter p a).size + 1

                                Per-dispatch-layer filtered growth lemmas #

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanDocumentStart_filtered_grows (s : Scanner.ScannerState) :
                                have p := fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder; (Array.filter p (Scanner.scanDocumentStart s).tokens).size ≥ (Array.filter p s.tokens).size + 1
                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanDocumentEnd_filtered_grows (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h : Scanner.scanDocumentEnd s = Except.ok s') :
                                have p := fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder; (Array.filter p s'.tokens).size ≥ (Array.filter p s.tokens).size + 1
                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.dispatchStructural_filtered_mono (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (h : Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchStructural s c = Except.ok (some s')) :
                                (Array.filter (fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder) s'.tokens).size ≥ (Array.filter (fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder) s.tokens).size
                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.dispatchFlowIndicators_filtered_grows (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (h : Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchFlowIndicators s c = Except.ok (some s')) :
                                (Array.filter (fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder) s'.tokens).size ≥ (Array.filter (fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder) s.tokens).size + 1
                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanBlockEntry_filtered_grows (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h : Scanner.scanBlockEntry s = Except.ok s') :
                                (Array.filter (fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder) s'.tokens).size ≥ (Array.filter (fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder) s.tokens).size + 1
                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanKey_filtered_grows (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h : Scanner.scanKey s = Except.ok s') :
                                (Array.filter (fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder) s'.tokens).size ≥ (Array.filter (fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder) s.tokens).size + 1
                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanValue_filtered_grows (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h : Scanner.scanValue s = Except.ok s') :
                                (Array.filter (fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder) s'.tokens).size ≥ (Array.filter (fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder) s.tokens).size + 1
                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.dispatchBlockIndicators_filtered_grows (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (h : Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchBlockIndicators s c = Except.ok (some s')) :
                                (Array.filter (fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder) s'.tokens).size ≥ (Array.filter (fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder) s.tokens).size + 1
                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.dispatchContent_new_not_placeholder (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (h : Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchContent s c = Except.ok s') (h_strict : s'.tokens.size ≥ s.tokens.size + 1) :
                                s'.tokens[s.tokens.size].val ≠ YamlToken.placeholder
                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.dispatchContent_filtered_grows (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (h : Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchContent s c = Except.ok s') :
                                (Array.filter (fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder) s'.tokens).size ≥ (Array.filter (fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder) s.tokens).size + 1

                                Main theorem: filtered growth through scanNextToken #

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanNextToken_filtered_grows (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h : Scanner.scanNextToken s = Except.ok (some s')) :
                                (Array.filter (fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder) s'.tokens).size ≥ (Array.filter (fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder) s.tokens).size + 1
                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.ScanChain_filtered_grows {s s' : Scanner.ScannerState} {n : Nat} (h_chain : ScanChain s n s') :
                                (Array.filter (fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder) s'.tokens).size ≥ (Array.filter (fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder) s.tokens).size + n

                                Through a ScanChain of n steps, the filtered token array grows by at least n.

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.ScanChain_filtered_prefix {s s' : Scanner.ScannerState} {n fl₀ : Nat} (h_fmc : FlowMonoChain fl₀ s n s') (h_sk : s.simpleKey.possible = false) (h_sync : s.simpleKeyStack.size ≥ s.flowLevel) (h_stack_floor : ∀ (j : Nat), fl₀ ≤ j → ∀ (hj : j < s.simpleKeyStack.size), s.simpleKeyStack[j].possible = true → s.simpleKeyStack[j].tokenIndex ≥ s.tokens.size) :
                                have p := fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder; ∃ (suffix : List (Positioned YamlToken)), (Array.filter p s'.tokens).toList = (Array.filter p s.tokens).toList ++ suffix

                                Through a FlowMonoChain, the filtered token array of the final state has the filtered array of the initial state as a prefix.

                                Uses FlowMonoChain_preserves_raw_prefix (which maintains SimpleKeyAboveFloor through the chain using the flow-level floor) composed with Array_filter_prefix_of_raw_prefix to lift raw index preservation to filtered-array prefix preservation.

                                Preconditions:

                                • FlowMonoChain fl₀ s n s': flow-monotone chain with floor fl₀
                                • h_sk: s.simpleKey.possible = false (no in-flight placeholder reservation)
                                • h_sync: s.simpleKeyStack.size ≥ s.flowLevel (stack/flow synchronized)
                                • h_stack_floor: stack entries at index ≥ fl₀ have tokenIndex ≥ s.tokens.size

                                Both call sites have fl₀ = s₁.flowLevel = 1 with s₁.simpleKeyStack.size = 1, making h_stack_floor vacuously true (no j satisfies 1 ≤ j < 1).

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.emitPairList_toList_ne_nil (p : YamlValue × YamlValue) (ps : List (YamlValue × YamlValue)) :
                                (Emit.emit.emitPairList (p :: ps)).toList ≠ []

                                emitPairList for non-empty pairs produces a non-empty string.

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanFlowSequenceEnd_tokens_eq (s : Scanner.ScannerState) :
                                (Scanner.scanFlowSequenceEnd s).tokens = s.tokens.push { pos := s.currentPos, val := YamlToken.flowSequenceEnd }

                                scanFlowSequenceEnd token array equation: pushes exactly one .flowSequenceEnd token.

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanFlowMappingEnd_tokens_eq (s : Scanner.ScannerState) :
                                (Scanner.scanFlowMappingEnd s).tokens = s.tokens.push { pos := s.currentPos, val := YamlToken.flowMappingEnd }

                                scanFlowMappingEnd token array equation: pushes exactly one .flowMappingEnd token.

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanNextToken_flow_close_seq_outermost_ext (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (hcorr : CouplingBridge.ScannerSurfCorr s { chars := [']'], col := s.col }) (h_flow : s.inFlow = true) (h_indent : s.currentIndent < 0) (h_col_pos : s.col > 0) (h_fl : s.flowLevel = 1) (h_dp : s.directivesPresent = false) :
                                have p := fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder; ∃ (s' : Scanner.ScannerState), Scanner.scanNextToken s = Except.ok (some s') ∧ s'.flowLevel = 0 ∧ s'.directivesPresent = false ∧ s'.peek? = none ∧ s'.indents = s.indents ∧ ∃ (tok : Positioned YamlToken), tok.val = YamlToken.flowSequenceEnd ∧ Array.filter p s'.tokens = (Array.filter p s.tokens).push tok

                                The close-bracket step for outermost ]: filtered token array is the input filtered array with .flowSequenceEnd appended.

                                Traces through saveSimpleKey (adds only placeholders, filtered out) → allowDirectives (no token change) → scanFlowSequenceEnd (appends .flowSequenceEnd which passes the placeholder filter).

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanNextToken_flow_close_mapping_outermost_ext (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (hcorr : CouplingBridge.ScannerSurfCorr s { chars := ['}'], col := s.col }) (h_flow : s.inFlow = true) (h_indent : s.currentIndent < 0) (h_col_pos : s.col > 0) (h_fl : s.flowLevel = 1) (h_dp : s.directivesPresent = false) :
                                have p := fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder; ∃ (s' : Scanner.ScannerState), Scanner.scanNextToken s = Except.ok (some s') ∧ s'.flowLevel = 0 ∧ s'.directivesPresent = false ∧ s'.peek? = none ∧ s'.indents = s.indents ∧ ∃ (tok : Positioned YamlToken), tok.val = YamlToken.flowMappingEnd ∧ Array.filter p s'.tokens = (Array.filter p s.tokens).push tok

                                The close-brace step for outermost }: filtered token array is the input filtered array with .flowMappingEnd appended.

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanFiltered_boundary_tokens (input : String) (tokens : Array (Positioned YamlToken)) (h : Scanner.scanFiltered input = Except.ok tokens) :
                                tokens.size ≥ 2 ∧ tokens[0]!.val = YamlToken.streamStart ∧ tokens[tokens.size - 1]!.val = YamlToken.streamEnd
                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanFlowSequenceStart_filtered (s : Scanner.ScannerState) :
                                have p := fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder; Array.filter p (Scanner.scanFlowSequenceStart s).tokens = (Array.filter p s.tokens).push { pos := s.currentPos, val := YamlToken.flowSequenceStart }

                                scanFlowSequenceStart filtered token equation: adds exactly one .flowSequenceStart.

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanFlowMappingStart_filtered (s : Scanner.ScannerState) :
                                have p := fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder; Array.filter p (Scanner.scanFlowMappingStart s).tokens = (Array.filter p s.tokens).push { pos := s.currentPos, val := YamlToken.flowMappingStart }

                                scanFlowMappingStart filtered token equation: adds exactly one .flowMappingStart.

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanFlowEntry_filtered (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h : Scanner.scanFlowEntry s = Except.ok s') :
                                have p := fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder; Array.filter p s'.tokens = (Array.filter p s.tokens).push { pos := s.currentPos, val := YamlToken.flowEntry }

                                scanFlowEntry filtered token equation (when it succeeds): adds exactly one .flowEntry.

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.ScanChain_deterministic {s s₁ s₂ : Scanner.ScannerState} {n : Nat} (h₁ : ScanChain s n s₁) (h₂ : ScanChain s n s₂) :
                                s₁ = s₂

                                ScanChain_deterministic: two chains with the same start state and step count reach the same final state (since scanNextToken is a function).

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.ScanChain.split {s s₁ s₂ : Scanner.ScannerState} {n₁ n₂ : Nat} (h₁ : ScanChain s n₁ s₁) (h_total : ScanChain s (n₁ + n₂) s₂) :
                                ScanChain s₁ n₂ s₂

                                ScanChain.split: decompose a chain into two consecutive sub-chains.

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.emitList_body_filtered_characterization (items : List YamlValue) (h_ne : items ≠ []) (h_all : ∀ (v : YamlValue), v ∈ items → EmitScansInFlow v) (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (rest : List Char) (h_corr : CouplingBridge.ScannerSurfCorr s { chars := (Emit.emit.emitList items).toList ++ rest, col := s.col }) (h_flow : s.inFlow = true) (h_fl : s.flowLevel > 0) (h_indent : s.currentIndent < 0) (h_col : s.col > 0) (h_ek : s.explicitKeyLine = none) (h_atol : AllTokensOnLine s s.line) (h_endline : EndLineOnLine s) (h_sk : s.simpleKey.possible = false) :
                                have p := fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder; have old_sz := (Array.filter p s.tokens).size; ∃ (n : Nat), ∃ (s' : Scanner.ScannerState), ScanChain s n s' ∧ CouplingBridge.ScannerSurfCorr s' { chars := rest, col := s'.col } ∧ s'.flowLevel = s.flowLevel ∧ s'.directivesPresent = s.directivesPresent ∧ s'.indents = s.indents ∧ s'.explicitKeyLine = s.explicitKeyLine ∧ s'.col > 0 ∧ s'.inFlow = true ∧ s'.currentIndent < 0 ∧ s'.line = s.line ∧ AllTokensOnLine s' s'.line ∧ EndLineOnLine s' ∧ s'.simpleKeyStack = s.simpleKeyStack ∧ FlowMonoChain s.flowLevel s n s' ∧ (old_sz < (Array.filter p s'.tokens).size ∧ ∀ (h : old_sz < (Array.filter p s'.tokens).size), (∃ (c : String), ∃ (sc : ScalarStyle), (Array.filter p s'.tokens)[old_sz].val = YamlToken.scalar c sc) ∨ (Array.filter p s'.tokens)[old_sz].val = YamlToken.flowSequenceStart ∨ (Array.filter p s'.tokens)[old_sz].val = YamlToken.flowMappingStart) ∧ ∀ (k : Nat), old_sz ≤ k → ∀ (h_hi : k < (Array.filter p s'.tokens).size), (Array.filter p s'.tokens)[k].val = YamlToken.flowEntry → ParserGrammable.flowBracketBalance (Array.filter p s'.tokens) old_sz k = 0 → k + 1 < (Array.filter p s'.tokens).size ∧ ∀ (h' : k + 1 < (Array.filter p s'.tokens).size), (∃ (c : String), ∃ (sc : ScalarStyle), (Array.filter p s'.tokens)[k + 1].val = YamlToken.scalar c sc) ∨ (Array.filter p s'.tokens)[k + 1].val = YamlToken.flowSequenceStart ∨ (Array.filter p s'.tokens)[k + 1].val = YamlToken.flowMappingStart

                                Body token characterization for emitList in flow context: (1) The first new filtered token (at position old_sz) is a content start. (2) After every OUTER-LEVEL .flowEntry (where bracket balance from old_sz to k is 0), the next filtered token is a content start.

                                These follow from emitList's structure: items separated by ", " (comma + space). Each item starts with emit v, whose first character (", [, or {) dispatches to scanDoubleQuoted, scanFlowSequenceStart, or scanFlowMappingStart — none of which emit .flowEntry or .key as their first filtered token.

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.emitPairList_body_filtered_characterization (pairs : List (YamlValue × YamlValue)) (h_ne : pairs ≠ []) (h_all_k : ∀ (p : YamlValue × YamlValue), p ∈ pairs → EmitScansInFlow p.fst) (h_all_v : ∀ (p : YamlValue × YamlValue), p ∈ pairs → EmitScansInFlow p.snd) (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (rest : List Char) (h_corr : CouplingBridge.ScannerSurfCorr s { chars := (Emit.emit.emitPairList pairs).toList ++ rest, col := s.col }) (h_flow : s.inFlow = true) (h_fl : s.flowLevel > 0) (h_indent : s.currentIndent < 0) (h_col : s.col > 0) (h_ek : s.explicitKeyLine = none) (h_atol : AllTokensOnLine s s.line) (h_endline : EndLineOnLine s) (h_sk : s.simpleKey.possible = false) :
                                have p := fun (t : Positioned YamlToken) => t.val != YamlToken.placeholder; have old_sz := (Array.filter p s.tokens).size; ∃ (n : Nat), ∃ (s' : Scanner.ScannerState), ScanChain s n s' ∧ CouplingBridge.ScannerSurfCorr s' { chars := rest, col := s'.col } ∧ s'.flowLevel = s.flowLevel ∧ s'.directivesPresent = s.directivesPresent ∧ s'.indents = s.indents ∧ s'.explicitKeyLine = s.explicitKeyLine ∧ s'.col > 0 ∧ s'.inFlow = true ∧ s'.currentIndent < 0 ∧ s'.line = s.line ∧ AllTokensOnLine s' s'.line ∧ EndLineOnLine s' ∧ s'.simpleKeyStack = s.simpleKeyStack ∧ FlowMonoChain s.flowLevel s n s' ∧ n ≥ 3 ∧ (old_sz < (Array.filter p s'.tokens).size ∧ ∀ (h : old_sz < (Array.filter p s'.tokens).size), (Array.filter p s'.tokens)[old_sz].val = YamlToken.key) ∧ ∀ (k : Nat), old_sz ≤ k → ∀ (h_hi : k < (Array.filter p s'.tokens).size), (Array.filter p s'.tokens)[k].val = YamlToken.flowEntry → ParserGrammable.flowBracketBalance (Array.filter p s'.tokens) old_sz k = 0 → k + 1 < (Array.filter p s'.tokens).size ∧ ∀ (h' : k + 1 < (Array.filter p s'.tokens).size), (Array.filter p s'.tokens)[k + 1].val = YamlToken.key

                                Body token characterization for emitPairList in flow context: (1) The chain has ≥ 3 steps (key handling + value indicator + value content). (2) The first new filtered token is .key (from saveSimpleKey + scanValuePrepare retroactively converting a placeholder when : is scanned). (3) After every OUTER-LEVEL .flowEntry (where bracket balance from old_sz to k is 0), the next filtered token is .key.

                                These follow from emitPairList's structure: each pair produces emit k ++ ": " ++ emit v, with pairs separated by ", ". The : triggers scanValuePrepare which converts the placeholder (saved by saveSimpleKey before scanning emit k) to .key. After each comma separator, the next pair starts with emit k again, preceded by saveSimpleKey.

                                IMPORTANT: The flowEntry pattern (part 3) is restricted to outer-level flowEntries (bracketBalance = 0). Inner flowEntries from nested sequences/mappings may be followed by content-start tokens rather than .key. The parser loop only visits outer-level flowEntries because parseNode consumes entire nested bracket groups.

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanFiltered_emitSeq_nonempty_structure (items : Array YamlValue) (tokens : Array (Positioned YamlToken)) (h_scan : Scanner.scanFiltered ("[" ++ Emit.emit.emitList items.toList ++ "]") = Except.ok tokens) (h_ne : items.toList ≠ []) (h_all_scan : ∀ (w : YamlValue), w ∈ items.toList → EmitScansInFlow w) :
                                tokens.size ≥ 5 ∧ tokens[0]!.val = YamlToken.streamStart ∧ tokens[tokens.size - 1]!.val = YamlToken.streamEnd ∧ tokens[1]!.val = YamlToken.flowSequenceStart ∧ tokens[tokens.size - 2]!.val = YamlToken.flowSequenceEnd ∧ ((∃ (c : String), ∃ (s : ScalarStyle), tokens[2]!.val = YamlToken.scalar c s) ∨ tokens[2]!.val = YamlToken.flowSequenceStart ∨ tokens[2]!.val = YamlToken.flowMappingStart) ∧ (∀ (k : Nat), 2 ≤ k → k < tokens.size - 2 → tokens[k]!.val = YamlToken.flowEntry → ParserGrammable.flowBracketBalance tokens 2 k = 0 → k + 1 ≤ tokens.size - 2 ∧ ((∃ (c : String), ∃ (s : ScalarStyle), tokens[k + 1]!.val = YamlToken.scalar c s) ∨ tokens[k + 1]!.val = YamlToken.flowSequenceStart ∨ tokens[k + 1]!.val = YamlToken.flowMappingStart)) ∧ ParserGrammable.ParseNodeFlowSeqOk tokens (tokens.size - 2) (4 * tokens.size + 4) 2

                                Token structure of scanFiltered ("[" ++ emitList items ++ "]") for non-empty items. Establishes boundary tokens, body token patterns, and parseNode success within the flow sequence body.

                                Requires EmitScansInFlow for each item to construct the scanner chain.

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.scanFiltered_emitMap_nonempty_structure (pairs : Array (YamlValue × YamlValue)) (tokens : Array (Positioned YamlToken)) (h_scan : Scanner.scanFiltered ("{" ++ Emit.emit.emitPairList pairs.toList ++ "}") = Except.ok tokens) (h_ne : pairs.toList ≠ []) (h_all_scan_k : ∀ (p : YamlValue × YamlValue), p ∈ pairs.toList → EmitScansInFlow p.fst) (h_all_scan_v : ∀ (p : YamlValue × YamlValue), p ∈ pairs.toList → EmitScansInFlow p.snd) :
                                tokens.size ≥ 7 ∧ tokens[0]!.val = YamlToken.streamStart ∧ tokens[tokens.size - 1]!.val = YamlToken.streamEnd ∧ tokens[1]!.val = YamlToken.flowMappingStart ∧ tokens[tokens.size - 2]!.val = YamlToken.flowMappingEnd ∧ tokens[2]!.val = YamlToken.key ∧ (∀ (k : Nat), 2 ≤ k → k < tokens.size - 2 → tokens[k]!.val = YamlToken.flowEntry → ParserGrammable.flowBracketBalance tokens 2 k = 0 → k + 1 ≤ tokens.size - 2 ∧ tokens[k + 1]!.val = YamlToken.key) ∧ ParserGrammable.ParseEntryFlowMapOk tokens (tokens.size - 2) (4 * tokens.size + 4) 2

                                Token structure of scanFiltered ("{" ++ emitPairList pairs ++ "}") for non-empty pairs. Establishes boundary tokens, body token patterns, and parseExplicitKey/parseFlowMappingValue success within the flow mapping body.

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.parseStream_emitSequence (style : CollectionStyle) (items : Array YamlValue) (tag anchor : Option String) {tokens : Array (Positioned YamlToken)} (h_scan : Scanner.scanFiltered (Emit.emit (YamlValue.sequence style items tag anchor)) = Except.ok tokens) (h_items : ∀ (i : Fin items.size), Grammar.Grammable items[i] (false || style == CollectionStyle.flow)) :
                                ∃ (docs : Array YamlDocument), TokenParser.parseStream tokens = Except.ok docs ∧ docs.size = 1

                                Combined scanner characterization and parser acceptance for flow sequences. Given that scanning the emitted sequence succeeds, the parser pipeline produces exactly one document.

                                • Empty case (items = #[]): Fully proven via native_decide on the concrete 4-token stream [streamStart, flowSequenceStart, flowSequenceEnd, streamEnd].
                                • Non-empty case: Requires parser fuel sufficiency for parseFlowSequenceLoop on well-bracketed tokens — each loop iteration consumes ≥1 token via parseNode, so fuel = 4 * tokens.size + 4 suffices. Currently sorry'd pending position monotonicity proof through parseNode dispatch.
                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.parseStream_emitMapping (style : CollectionStyle) (pairs : Array (YamlValue × YamlValue)) (tag anchor : Option String) {tokens : Array (Positioned YamlToken)} (h_scan : Scanner.scanFiltered (Emit.emit (YamlValue.mapping style pairs tag anchor)) = Except.ok tokens) (hk : ∀ (i : Fin pairs.size), Grammar.Grammable pairs[i].fst (false || style == CollectionStyle.flow)) (hv : ∀ (i : Fin pairs.size), Grammar.Grammable pairs[i].snd (false || style == CollectionStyle.flow)) :
                                ∃ (docs : Array YamlDocument), TokenParser.parseStream tokens = Except.ok docs ∧ docs.size = 1

                                Combined scanner characterization and parser acceptance for flow mappings. Analogous to parseStream_emitSequence but for emit (.mapping ...).

                                • Empty case (pairs = #[]): Fully proven via native_decide on the concrete 4-token stream [streamStart, flowMappingStart, flowMappingEnd, streamEnd].
                                • Non-empty case: Requires parser fuel sufficiency for parseFlowMappingLoop on well-bracketed tokens. Currently sorry'd pending position monotonicity proof.
                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.parseStream_accepts_emit_tokens (v : YamlValue) (hg : Grammar.Grammable v false) (tokens : Array (Positioned YamlToken)) (h_scan : Scanner.scanFiltered (Emit.emit v) = Except.ok tokens) :
                                ∃ (docs : Array YamlDocument), TokenParser.parseStream tokens = Except.ok docs

                                Parse acceptance (Step 2): The parser accepts the token sequence produced by scanning canonical emitter output.

                                Given that the scanner successfully tokenized emitter output, parseStream also succeeds. The emitter's restricted output format (double-quoted scalars, flow-only collections, single implicit document) avoids all parseStream error conditions.

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.emit_produces_single_document (v : YamlValue) (hg : Grammar.Grammable v false) (docs : Array YamlDocument) (h : TokenParser.parseYamlRaw (Emit.emit v) = Except.ok docs) :
                                docs.size = 1

                                Single document: The canonical emitter's output produces exactly one document when parsed.

                                The emitter generates a single implicit document (no --- markers, no multiple-document output), so parseStreamLoop produces #[doc]. This is needed for the universal round-trip theorem which asserts docs.size = 1.

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.emit_parse_succeeds (v : YamlValue) (hg : Grammar.Grammable v false) :
                                ∃ (docs : Array YamlDocument), TokenParser.parseYamlRaw (Emit.emit v) = Except.ok docs

                                Full pipeline (raw): The canonical emitter's output parses successfully through parseYamlRaw.

                                Composes Step 1 (emit_produces_valid_yaml: scanner acceptance) with Step 2 (parseStream_accepts_emit_tokens: parser acceptance) via parseYamlRaw_pipeline (scan + parse → pipeline success).

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.emit_parseYaml_succeeds (v : YamlValue) (hg : Grammar.Grammable v false) :
                                ∃ (docs : Array YamlDocument), TokenParser.parseYaml (Emit.emit v) = Except.ok docs

                                Full pipeline (with compose): Emitter output parses successfully through parseYaml, which resolves aliases via YamlDocument.compose.

                                Since the emitter produces no aliases (Grammable excludes .alias nodes), compose is effectively the identity on values, but the types require going through this step.

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.contentEq_sequence_items (style₁ style₂ : CollectionStyle) (items₁ items₂ : Array YamlValue) (tag₁ tag₂ anchor₁ anchor₂ : Option String) :
                                Emit.contentEq (YamlValue.sequence style₁ items₁ tag₁ anchor₁) (YamlValue.sequence style₂ items₂ tag₂ anchor₂) = (items₁.size == items₂.size && Emit.contentEq.contentEqList items₁.toList items₂.toList)
                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.contentEq_mapping_pairs (style₁ style₂ : CollectionStyle) (pairs₁ pairs₂ : Array (YamlValue × YamlValue)) (tag₁ tag₂ anchor₁ anchor₂ : Option String) :
                                Emit.contentEq (YamlValue.mapping style₁ pairs₁ tag₁ anchor₁) (YamlValue.mapping style₂ pairs₂ tag₂ anchor₂) = (pairs₁.size == pairs₂.size && Emit.contentEq.contentEqPairList pairs₁.toList pairs₂.toList)
                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.contentEq_seq_style_irrel (style : CollectionStyle) (items : Array YamlValue) (tag anchor : Option String) (v : YamlValue) :
                                Emit.contentEq (YamlValue.sequence style items tag anchor) v = Emit.contentEq (YamlValue.sequence CollectionStyle.flow items) v
                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.contentEq_map_style_irrel (style : CollectionStyle) (pairs : Array (YamlValue × YamlValue)) (tag anchor : Option String) (v : YamlValue) :
                                Emit.contentEq (YamlValue.mapping style pairs tag anchor) v = Emit.contentEq (YamlValue.mapping CollectionStyle.flow pairs) v
                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.emit_roundtrip_sequence_content_eq {inFlow : Bool} (style : CollectionStyle) (items : Array YamlValue) (tag anchor : Option String) (raw_docs : Array YamlDocument) (h_raw : TokenParser.parseYamlRaw (Emit.emit (YamlValue.sequence style items tag anchor)) = Except.ok raw_docs) (h_size : raw_docs.size = 1) (h_items : ∀ (i : Fin items.size), Grammar.Grammable items[i] (inFlow || style == CollectionStyle.flow)) (ih : ∀ (i : Fin items.size) (raw_docs' : Array YamlDocument), TokenParser.parseYamlRaw (Emit.emit items[i]) = Except.ok raw_docs' → raw_docs'.size = 1 → Emit.contentEq items[i] (Array.map YamlDocument.compose raw_docs')[0]!.value = true) :
                                Emit.contentEq (YamlValue.sequence style items tag anchor) (Array.map YamlDocument.compose raw_docs)[0]!.value = true

                                Proves that parsing the emitted tokens for a flow sequence recovers a content-equivalent sequence.

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.emit_roundtrip_mapping_content_eq {inFlow : Bool} (style : CollectionStyle) (pairs : Array (YamlValue × YamlValue)) (tag anchor : Option String) (raw_docs : Array YamlDocument) (h_raw : TokenParser.parseYamlRaw (Emit.emit (YamlValue.mapping style pairs tag anchor)) = Except.ok raw_docs) (h_size : raw_docs.size = 1) (hk : ∀ (i : Fin pairs.size), Grammar.Grammable pairs[i].fst (inFlow || style == CollectionStyle.flow)) (hv : ∀ (i : Fin pairs.size), Grammar.Grammable pairs[i].snd (inFlow || style == CollectionStyle.flow)) (ihk : ∀ (i : Fin pairs.size) (raw_docs' : Array YamlDocument), TokenParser.parseYamlRaw (Emit.emit pairs[i].fst) = Except.ok raw_docs' → raw_docs'.size = 1 → Emit.contentEq pairs[i].fst (Array.map YamlDocument.compose raw_docs')[0]!.value = true) (ihv : ∀ (i : Fin pairs.size) (raw_docs' : Array YamlDocument), TokenParser.parseYamlRaw (Emit.emit pairs[i].snd) = Except.ok raw_docs' → raw_docs'.size = 1 → Emit.contentEq pairs[i].snd (Array.map YamlDocument.compose raw_docs')[0]!.value = true) :
                                Emit.contentEq (YamlValue.mapping style pairs tag anchor) (Array.map YamlDocument.compose raw_docs)[0]!.value = true

                                Proves that parsing the emitted tokens for a flow mapping recovers a content-equivalent mapping.

                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.emit_roundtrip_content_eq (v : YamlValue) {b : Bool} (hg : Grammar.Grammable v b) (raw_docs : Array YamlDocument) (h_raw : TokenParser.parseYamlRaw (Emit.emit v) = Except.ok raw_docs) (h_size : raw_docs.size = 1) :
                                Emit.contentEq v (Array.map YamlDocument.compose raw_docs)[0]!.value = true

                                Content fidelity: Parsing canonical emitter output recovers content equivalent to the original value.

                                The canonical emitter produces double-quoted scalars, flow-style collections, and no aliases/tags/anchors. Parsing this output yields values with the same string content for scalars and the same tree structure for collections, differing only in style annotations. Since contentEq ignores style, the parsed result is content-equivalent to the original.

                                Proof strategy: Structural induction on v:

                                • Scalar: escapeString round-trips through the scanner's collectDoubleQuotedLoop + processEscape, recovering the original content string. contentEq ignores scalar style.
                                • Sequence: By IH each element round-trips content-equivalently. The parser reconstructs the list from flow tokens.
                                • Mapping: By IH each key/value round-trips content-equivalently. The parser reconstructs pairs from flow tokens.
                                source
                                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.EmitterScannability.universal_roundtrip (v : YamlValue) (hg : Grammar.Grammable v false) :
                                ∃ (docs : Array YamlDocument), TokenParser.parseYaml (Emit.emit v) = Except.ok docs ∧ docs.size = 1 ∧ Emit.contentEq v docs[0]!.value = true

                                Universal round-trip: For every grammable YAML value, emitting it and re-parsing produces a single document whose value is content-equivalent to the original.

                                This is the main theorem of v0.4.7 (Phase E). It composes:

                                • Step 1: emit_produces_valid_yaml (scanner accepts emitter output)
                                • Step 2: parseStream_accepts_emit_tokens (parser accepts scanned tokens)
                                • Step 3a: emit_produces_single_document (exactly one document)
                                • Step 3b: emit_roundtrip_content_eq (content fidelity)
